name: Serrejón y Almaraz desde Casatejada — circular por el Embalse de Arrocampo
region_intro: Road cycling around Casatejada offers diverse terrain within the Monfragüe Biosphere Reserve, characterized by extensive dehesas and pastureland. The region features rolling hills and Mediterranean forests, providing varied cycling experiences. Routes often explore the scenic surroundings of the Arrocampo Reservoir, known for its wetlands and bird habitats. The broader Cáceres area includes river valleys and unique granite rock formations, contributing to a varied landscape for road cyclists. Best road cycling routes around Casatejada The most popular road cycling route is Almaraz to Puerto de Campillo — circular via Puerto de Miravete and Deleitosa, a 40.2 miles (64.7 km) trail that takes 4 hours 0 minutes to complete. This difficult route includes significant elevation gain through the region's varied topography. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Serrejón y Almaraz desde Casatejada — circular por el Embalse de Arrocampo, a moderate 42.9 miles (69.1 km) path. This route offers views of the Arrocampo Reservoir and passes through local villages. Local road cyclists also love the Talayuela - Tiétar River Loop, a 29.6 miles (47.6 km) trail leading through the Tiétar River area, often completed in about 1 hour 59 minutes. Road cycling around Casatejada is defined by dehesas, the Arrocampo Reservoir, and Mediterranean forests. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to different ability levels. The routes in Casatejada are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 2 reviews. More than 600 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Casatejada's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Casatejada?
A: There are over 60 road cycling routes in the Casatejada area, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels. You'll find routes traversing dehesas, Mediterranean forests, and along the scenic Arrocampo Reservoir.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling near Casatejada?
A: Road cycling around Casatejada offers diverse terrain, from the rolling hills and extensive dehesas of the Monfragüe Biosphere Reserve to routes exploring the wetlands around the Arrocampo Reservoir. The broader Cáceres region also features river valleys and unique granite rock formations, providing varied landscapes and challenges.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Casatejada?
A: Yes, Casatejada offers several routes suitable for beginners. There are 9 easy routes available, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1353230255/talayuela-tietar-river-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Talayuela - Tiétar River Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 29.6 miles (47.6 km) long, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1371635024/casatejada-to-serrejon-and-almaraz-circular-by-the-embalse-de-arrocampo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casatejada to Serrejón and Almaraz — circular by the Embalse de Arrocampo&lt;/a&gt;, a 36.5 km route.

Q: What are some moderate road cycling routes around Casatejada?
A: For those seeking a moderate challenge, Casatejada has 35 routes. A popular choice is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1371337686/serrejon-y-almaraz-desde-casatejada-circular-por-el-embalse-de-arrocampo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serrejón y Almaraz desde Casatejada — circular por el Embalse de Arrocampo&lt;/a&gt;, a 42.9 miles (69.1 km) path offering views of the Arrocampo Reservoir and passing through local villages. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1371347441/casatejada-serrejon-and-toril-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casatejada, Serrejón, and Toril Loop&lt;/a&gt;, spanning 53.4 km.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes with significant elevation gain?
A: Yes, for experienced cyclists, there are 21 difficult routes around Casatejada. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1371626413/almaraz-to-puerto-de-campillo-circular-via-puerto-de-miravete-and-deleitosa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almaraz to Puerto de Campillo — circular via Puerto de Miravete and Deleitosa&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 40.2 miles (64.7 km) trail with over 1200 meters of elevation gain, taking you through the region's varied topography.

Q: What interesting landmarks or attractions can I see along road cycling routes?
A: Many routes offer opportunities to explore the region's heritage. You might pass through villages like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5177421" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Toril&lt;/a&gt;, see religious buildings such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5393762" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de San Blas&lt;/a&gt;, or historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5696297" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Granite Pillar of Almaraz&lt;/a&gt;. The area also features castles like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5583007" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Belvís de Monroy&lt;/a&gt;, offering scenic viewpoints.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available from Casatejada?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes around Casatejada are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1371337686/serrejon-y-almaraz-desde-casatejada-circular-por-el-embalse-de-arrocampo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serrejón y Almaraz desde Casatejada — circular por el Embalse de Arrocampo&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1371347441/casatejada-serrejon-and-toril-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casatejada, Serrejón, and Toril Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Casatejada?
A: The region's Mediterranean climate generally makes spring and autumn ideal for road cycling, with milder temperatures. While specific weather data isn't provided, these seasons typically offer comfortable conditions for exploring the dehesas and natural reserves without the intense heat of summer.

Q: Can I expect to see wildlife while cycling in Casatejada?
A: Given Casatejada's location within the Monfragüe Biosphere Reserve and near the Arrocampo Reservoir, which is a significant bird habitat, you have a good chance of encountering diverse wildlife. The dehesas and Mediterranean forests are home to various species, making for an enriching cycling experience.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Casatejada?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 2 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the scenic beauty of the Monfragüe Biosphere Reserve, and the peaceful routes around the Arrocampo Reservoir.

Q: Are there any amenities like cafes or places to stop along the routes?
A: While specific cafe stops are not detailed for every route, many routes pass through local villages like Serrejón, Almaraz, and Toril. These villages often provide opportunities for refreshments and amenities, allowing for convenient breaks during your ride.

Q: Is there parking available near the road cycling routes in Casatejada?
A: While specific parking information for each route start is not provided, Casatejada itself and the surrounding villages typically offer public parking options. It's advisable to check local parking availability in the starting village of your chosen route.
tips: Casatejada is a large town located on an important transport network and of singular natural beauty, the Monfragüe National Park Biosphere Reserve. Ride a bike through the town, rest in Plaza España and admire the Church of San Pedro.

Between Saucedilla and Almaraz you reach the incredible bird paradise, the Embalse de Arrocampo Ornithological Natural Park, an incredible area to explore by bicycle at the eastern end of the Monfragüe National Park.

Almaraz is located near the Tagus River and the Almaraz reservoir. The Iglesia San Andrés dominates the horizon and together with the medieval Castle of Almaraz they represent the rich heritage of Alamaraz. Today, Almaraz is best known for its nuclear power plant.

Surrounded by oak forest plantations, the small town of Serrejón is located in the foothills of the Sierra de Serrejón, on the eastern edge of the Monfragüe National Park. If you are passing through the town, stop to see the town hall square or the village church.
